content.full_text:
February 3, 1978

Lawrence J. Kowalski  
City Clerk  
City of Martinez  
225 Henrietta Street  
Martinez, California 94553

Dear Jack:

In your letter to the Commission of January 24, 1978, you asked whether or not Diamond International is allowed to advertise, solicit or actively participate in the annual conference of the City Clerk's Association if they provide refreshments at a reception prior to the banquet.

There is no prohibition under the Political Reform Act covering Diamond International's participation in your conference. The only possible ramification might be one of a disclosure nature depending on the conflict of interest code under which each of the individual clerks is required to file. There may be a requirement to disclose a gift received from Diamond International in the form of food or beverage served at the reception. However, most codes have a $25 threshold for reporting gifts and I doubt very much that even if city clerks are required to report gifts from entities which do business in their jurisdictions that the value of the gift received from Diamond International would be $25 or more (the amount included in most of the codes).

Legislators also would not be required to report receipt of a gift from your association for the same reason. Only gifts which total $25 or more must be disclosed.

It doesn't look like there are problems in either of these two areas.

Please let me know if I can be of any additional assistance.

Best regards,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

January 24, 1978

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Gina:

Thank you for your confirmation to participate in our Annual Conference of the City Clerks' Association of California.

If you recall, earlier I inquired as to whether the legislators who may attend our Conference as our guests, would be required to report the monetary value of the banquet, and your response was "no, inasmuch as this is considered protocol, and not political."

Another question, Diamond International has offered to provide the wherewithall for the reception prior to the banquet, and we are still working out details in this regard. If we can agree, Diamond would not be allowed to advertise, solicit or actively participate in this function. Would this be required to be reported? An early response will be appreciated.

Best regards,

Lawrence J. Kowalski  
City Clerk of Martinez
